paper_title: Study Site, Nuclear DNA Extraction and Sequencing

methods.description: Analysis of mitochondrial cytochrome b (cyt b) dataset from P. mexicanus individuals to study biogeographical patterns.

steps.description: We here analyzed a mitochondrial cytochrome b (cyt b) dataset (1113 bp fragment) of 186 P. mexicanus individuals from 45 localities from southern Mexico and Central America obtained in [20].
steps.searchable_summary: analyze mitochondrial cytochrome b dataset from GenBank

methods.description: Amplification and sequencing of Growth Hormone Receptor (GHR) gene to complement mitochondrial DNA results.

steps.description: We amplified and sequenced an 829 bp fragment of the Growth Hormone Receptor (GHR), using primers GHR1f and GHRend1f.
steps.searchable_summary: amplify and sequence GHR gene fragment

steps.description: Amplifications were performed in 25 µL reaction volume containing 50-10 ng template DNA, buffer 10×, 1 unit of Taq DNA polymerase, 2 mM of MgCl2, 200 µM of each dNTP and 0.4 µM of each primer.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare PCR reaction for DNA amplification

steps.description: The PCR cycling protocol was 5 min of initial denaturation at 94°C, followed by 35 cycles of 15 s at 94°C, 1 min at 60°C and 1.5 min at 72°C, and a final extension of 10 min at 72°C.
We used agarose gels (1.5%) dyed with ethidium bromide to visualize amplifications.
steps.searchable_summary: visualize DNA amplifications using agarose gel electrophoresis

steps.description: PCR products were purified and analyzed in an ABI3730xl DNA analyzer.
steps.searchable_summary: purify and analyze PCR products using DNA analyzer

methods.description: Inference of GHR haplotypes and phylogenetic analysis using maximum-likelihood and Bayesian methods.

steps.description: GHR haplotypes were inferred with a maximum-likelihood method implemented in PHASE v.2.1.1.
steps.searchable_summary: infer GHR haplotypes using PHASE software

steps.description: A multiple alignment was conducted with ClustalX-2.
steps.searchable_summary: perform multiple sequence alignment using ClustalX

steps.description: To select the best-fitted nucleotide evolution model we used jModelTest v.2.1.3 and the Akaike Information Criterion (AIC).
steps.searchable_summary: select nucleotide evolution model using jModelTest and AIC

steps.description: We conducted a phylogenetic Maximum Likelihood (ML) analysis with the program PhyML v.3.0.
steps.searchable_summary: perform ML phylogenetic analysis using PhyML

steps.description: A Bayesian Inference (BI) phylogenetic analysis was completed with MrBayes v.3.1.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Bayesian phylogenetic analysis using MrBayes

steps.description: Convergence and stationarity were visualized with Tracer v.1.7.1 with 25% generations discarded as burn-in.
steps.searchable_summary: check convergence and stationarity using Tracer

steps.description: Topologies were visualized and edited with FigTree v.1.4.0.
